title: claim (v1)
description: A substrate-grade CLAIM block. Consumed by tier_1_claim_runner.py which routes each claim through CLAIM/FALSIFY/PROMOTE using one of seven verifier types. Per the design at pivot/claim_stack_design_2026-05-11.md and Aporia adjudication 2026-05-12. The schema is producer-agnostic: hand-authored (Aporia) and mined (extractor) claims share the same shape; provenance is descriptive in source_report.

id.title: Claim ID
id.description: CLAIM-<category-prefix>-NNNNN format. category-prefix groups related claims (e.g. 'frontier', 'calibration-knots', 'boundary-T4', 'substrate-self'). Uppercase letters allowed in the prefix to accommodate catalog references (T4 = T#4). Substrate's content-addressed Symbol IDs assigned at PROMOTE; this id is the human-readable handle.

claim_category.title: Claim category for sampling discipline
claim_category.description: Per claim_stack_design §3 + Aporia Mod Q5: frontier_survey + calibration + boundary authored by Aporia; substrate_self authored by Techne. 'other' reserved for outside-typology claims pending category assignment.

claim_text.title: Natural-language claim statement
claim_text.description: The proposition being claimed, in natural language. Minimum 20 characters discourages trivial / placeholder claims at write time.

expected_verifier_primary.title: Primary verifier dispatch
expected_verifier_primary.description: Which of the seven verifier functions in tier_1_claim_runner.py routes this claim. Choose conservatively; manual_review is the escape hatch when no automated verifier fits.

expected_verifier_fallback.title: Optional fallback verifier
expected_verifier_fallback.description: When primary verifier returns inconclusive / transient_failure, the runner attempts the fallback. Same enum as primary.

expected_verdict.title: Author-asserted expected verdict
expected_verdict.description: What the author believes the verifier should return. Disagreement between expected and actual at runtime is informative (substrate-grade catch). Per Aporia Mod Q3: 'open' is allowed and maps to kernel's INCONCLUSIVE outcome class; conditional maps to INCONCLUSIVE with caveats metadata. No new kernel verdict added (no contract change).

ground_truth_source.title: Citation or dataset reference for ground truth
ground_truth_source.description: How the claim's truth/falsity is established. Either an arXiv ID / DOI (for citation-bearing claims) or free-form dataset reference (for catalog / training-anchor claims). Required even for expected_verdict=open (captures how the open status is known). Uses anyOf (not oneOf) because arXiv strings validate against both branches and we don't require exclusivity — the verifier dispatch examines the string content directly.

anyOf.description: Free-form when no arXiv/DOI applies (e.g. 'KnotInfo 2024-12 snapshot, knot 3_1' or 'T#4 catalog entry — exact value unknown in (19, 23)').

trust_tier.description: Substrate-grade trust tier inherited from ground_truth_source. Per Rule C (claim_stack_design §5): ml_predicted claims must not exceed 10% of any batch; analytically_proven + numerically_certified should be >=70%.

source_report.description: Provenance string. Hand-authored: 'AA-001 anti_anchor false_form'. Mined: 'extracted from <path> line N via extractor v0.1'. Producer-agnostic; the runner doesn't care which.

parent_block.title: Parent substrate_block reference
parent_block.description: When derived from another substrate_block (e.g. anti_anchor becoming a paired claim couplet), references the source block by id. Cross-reference checked at validate time when the parent ID type is known.

paired_claim_id.title: Paired claim (for couplets)
paired_claim_id.description: When this claim is part of a couplet (e.g. false_form/true_form derived from an anti_anchor), references the partner. Both claims point at each other. Train/eval split MUST keep paired claims together (anti-leakage).

prompt_template.title: Natural-language form for Learner Q&A
prompt_template.description: Template a Learner-Tester would use to ask the question. Required for calibration claims (Aporia adjudication Q2); optional for others (claim_text often suffices). Supports {placeholder} interpolation for parametric claims.

expected_answer_shape.title: Expected answer type description
expected_answer_shape.description: Free-form Python-type-annotation-style description of the expected answer shape. Useful for calibration claims; ignored otherwise.

verifier_args.title: Opaque verifier-specific arguments
verifier_args.description: Dict of arguments passed to the verifier dispatch (e.g. mpmath_compute receives {dps: 60, tolerance: 1e-30}; catalog_lookup receives {catalog_source: 'lmfdb'}). Schema is opaque; each verifier validates its own args internally.

stratification_rule.title: Calibration sampling stratification rule (Aporia Mod Q2)
stratification_rule.description: When this calibration claim was sampled from a parent training_anchor's instance set, this string documents the deterministic sampling rule for reproducibility. Examples: 'first 10 prime knots <= 8 crossings', 'every 30th LMFDB elliptic curve in conductor range [1, 10000]'. Optional; required only for calibration claims that subsample a parent anchor's instance set.

caveats.title: Conditionality / qualifier text
caveats.description: Free-form paragraph capturing measure-zero exceptions, GRH-conditionality, expected drift, paired-claim handling, etc. Required when expected_verdict is 'conditional' (the conditionality must be named); strongly recommended when expected_verdict is 'open' (how is the open status itself established).

examples.claim_text: Bürgisser-Ikenmeyer-Panova killed GCT entirely; occurrence obstructions for det/padded-perm are still a viable path to VP vs VNP separation.
examples.caveats: Paired with CLAIM-frontier-00002 which carries the TRUE form including the multiplicity / vanishing-ideal / outside-orbit / equivariant carve-outs.

examples.claim_text: The Alexander polynomial of the trefoil knot 3_1 is t**2 - t + 1 in canonical form (constant term +1).
examples.source_report: extracted from anchor-knots-001 training_anchor instance 1 via extractor v0.1
examples.prompt_template: Compute the Alexander polynomial of the knot 3_1 (trefoil). Normalize to canonical form (constant term = +1).
examples.expected_answer_shape: sympy.Poly over ZZ in variable t
examples.stratification_rule: first 10 prime knots <= 8 crossings, KnotInfo deterministic order

examples.claim_text: R(M<3>) <= 18 (the matrix multiplication tensor M<3> has rank at most 18).
examples.ground_truth_source: T#4 catalog entry — Landsberg lower bound R(M<3>) >= 19
examples.source_report: T#4 catalog entry, falsification probe (violates lower bound)
examples.caveats: Any claim of R(M<3>) <= 18 contradicts the established lower bound of 19. Substrate MUST emit FALSIFIED here; if it doesn't, that's a substrate failure surfacing a verifier gap.
